title: Vibration of Effects
text: R package estimating distribution effect sizes p-values due model specification choices. Given base exposure-outcome model, VoE systematically fits (random sample ) possible covariate adjustment subsets visualizes resulting variability estimates statistical significance.

title: Vibration of Effects
text: Patel CJ, Burford B, Ioannidis JPA. Assessment vibration effects due model specification can demonstrate instability observational associations. Journal Clinical Epidemiology. 2015;68(9):1046-1058.

title: Vibration of Effects

what: Example: Is serum cadmium associated with mortality?
title: Vibration of Effects
text: association serum cadmium mortality depends heavily confounders adjust . VoE quantifies instability fitting every possible combination adjustors summarizing spread effect sizes p-values. large adjustor sets, use conductVibrationSample() randomly sample model specifications instead exhaustive enumeration:
code: library(voe) library(survival)  ## 1. Load NHANES 1999-2004 mortality data --------------------------------- load("data/nhanes9904_VoE.Rdata")  dat <- mainTab[, c("MORTSTAT", "WTMEC4YR", "PERMTH_EXM", "SES_LEVEL",                     "RIDAGEYR", "male", "area", "LBXBCD",                     "current_past_smoking", "any_cad", "any_ht",                     "any_diabetes", "education", "RIDRETH1")] dat <- dat[complete.cases(dat), ]  ## 2. Define the base model and candidate adjustors ------------------------ ##    The base model always includes age, sex, and the exposure (cadmium). ##    The 7 candidate adjustors will be toggled on/off in every combination. basemodel <- Surv(PERMTH_EXM, MORTSTAT) ~ scale(I(log(LBXBCD))) +   RIDAGEYR + male + cluster(area)  adjustors <- ~ factor(current_past_smoking) + factor(SES_LEVEL) +   any_cad + any_ht + any_diabetes + factor(education) + factor(RIDRETH1)  ## 3. Run the vibration ---------------------------------------------------- ##    With 7 adjustors there are 2^7 - 1 = 127 models — runs in seconds. vib <- conductVibration(basemodel, dat, adjustors,                          family = "cox", weights = dat$WTMEC4YR)  ## 4. Summarize ------------------------------------------------------------ ##    RHR (relative hazard ratio) and RP (relative p-value) capture how ##    much the estimate and significance shift across model specifications. summ <- summary_vibration(vib$vibFrame, vib$bicFrame) summ$summary #>       HR_01    HR_50    HR_99  pvalue_01  pvalue_50  pvalue_99   rHR  rPvalue #>  1   1.03     1.12     1.18   2.8e-07    0.0003     0.19       1.15  5.83  ## 5. Visualize ------------------------------------------------------------ ##    Volcano plot: each point is one model specification. ##    Red line traces the median estimate at each k (number of adjustors). plot_vibration_cox(vib, type = "contour", alpha = 0.3)  ##    Color by whether smoking is in the model (adjustor #1): plot_vibration_cox(vib, type = "contour", alpha = 0.3, adjustment_num = 1) ## Sample 1000 of 2^13 - 1 = 8,191 possible models, using 4 cores vib <- conductVibrationSample(basemodel, dat, adjustors,                                family = "cox", n_models = 1000, n_cores = 4,                                weights = dat$WTMEC4YR)

title: Vibration of Effects
text: Cox proportional hazards (family = "cox") – primary use case; supports cluster() robust SEs survey weights Linear regression (family = "gaussian") Logistic regression (family = "binomial")

title: Vibration of Effects
text: vibFrame: data frame estimate, SE, z-statistic, p-value, combination index, factor level per model bicFrame: BIC effective degrees freedom per model Volcano plots: HR (x-axis) vs -log10(p-value) (y-axis), median trajectory across k highlighted red

title: Vibration of Effects
code: R/                       # Package source   vibration.R            # Core VoE computation (exhaustive + sampling)   plot_vibration.R       # Volcano and contour plots (ggplot2)   post_process.R         # Summary statistics (RHR, RP, Janus effect) man/                     # Auto-generated documentation  vibration/               # Original standalone scripts nhanes_vibration/        # Cluster-scale batch pipeline data/                    # NHANES dataset (nhanes9904_VoE.Rdata) jce_results/             # Figures and tables from the JCE paper

title: Vibration of Effects
text: Required: survival, MASS, rlang Suggested: ggplot2 (plotting), RColorBrewer (color palettes), parallel (multi-core sampling)

title: Exhaustive Vibration of Effects — conductVibration
text: Fits possible covariate adjustment subsets (kMin kMax adjustors) collects resulting coefficient estimates, p-values, BIC values.

title: Exhaustive Vibration of Effects — conductVibration
code: conductVibration(   base_formula,   dataFrame,   adjustby,   family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "cox"),   kMin = NULL,   kMax = NULL,   print_progress = TRUE,   ... )

title: Exhaustive Vibration of Effects — conductVibration
text: base_formula formula specifying base model. dataFrame data frame containing variables. adjustby formula character vector candidate adjustment variables. family One "gaussian", "binomial", "cox". kMin Minimum number adjustors (default 1). kMax Maximum number adjustors (default length(adjustby) - 1). print_progress Logical; print progress console. ... Additional arguments passed run_model().

title: Exhaustive Vibration of Effects — conductVibration
text: list components vibFrame, bicFrame, combinations, adjust, family, base_formula.

what: Compute VoE for a fixed number of adjustors — conductVibrationForK
title: Compute VoE for a fixed number of adjustors — conductVibrationForK
text: Fits combinations k covariates adjustby added base_formula collects coefficient estimates, p-values, BIC.

title: Compute VoE for a fixed number of adjustors — conductVibrationForK
code: conductVibrationForK(   base_formula,   dataFrame,   adjustby,   k = 1,   family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "cox"),   print_progress = TRUE,   ... )

title: Compute VoE for a fixed number of adjustors — conductVibrationForK
text: base_formula formula specifying base model (exposure + forced covariates). dataFrame data frame containing variables. adjustby formula character vector candidate adjustment variables. k Number adjustors include model. family One "gaussian", "binomial", "cox". print_progress Logical; print progress console. ... Additional arguments passed run_model().

title: Compute VoE for a fixed number of adjustors — conductVibrationForK
text: list components vibration, bic, k, combinations, family, base_formula, adjust.

title: Sampling-Based Vibration of Effects — conductVibrationSample
text: Instead fitting 2^n - 1 covariate subsets, draws random sample n_models adjustment combinations. Supports multi-core parallelization via parallel::mclapply().

title: Sampling-Based Vibration of Effects — conductVibrationSample
code: conductVibrationSample(   base_formula,   dataFrame,   adjustby,   family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "cox"),   n_models = 1000,   n_cores = 1,   print_progress = TRUE,   ... )

title: Sampling-Based Vibration of Effects — conductVibrationSample
text: base_formula formula specifying base model. dataFrame data frame containing variables. adjustby formula character vector candidate adjustment variables. family One "gaussian", "binomial", "cox". n_models Number random covariate subsets sample (default 1000). n_cores Number cores parallel execution (default 1). print_progress Logical; print progress console. ... Additional arguments passed run_model().

title: Sampling-Based Vibration of Effects — conductVibrationSample
text: list components vibFrame, bicFrame, combinations, adjust, family, base_formula, n_models_requested, n_models_fit.

title: Sampling-Based Vibration of Effects — conductVibrationSample
text: Falls back conductVibration() automatically n_models exceeds total number possible subsets.

what: Plot VoE for Cox models — plot_vibration_cox
title: Plot VoE for Cox models — plot_vibration_cox
text: Creates volcano plot hazard ratios vs -log10(p-value) VoE analysis. Two plot types available: hexbin ("bin") contour ("contour").

title: Plot VoE for Cox models — plot_vibration_cox
code: plot_vibration_cox(   vibObj,   type = c("bin", "contour"),   factor_num = 1,   adjustment_num = NA,   ... )

title: Plot VoE for Cox models — plot_vibration_cox
text: vibObj VoE result object (output conductVibration() conductVibrationSample()). type Plot type: "bin" hexbin "contour" contour plot. factor_num Factor level plot (default 1). adjustment_num NA, highlights models include adjustment variable index. ... Additional arguments passed plotting function.

title: Plot VoE for Cox models — plot_vibration_cox
